scenario_results.reason: Normal home health compliance support produced an evidence-backed disposition tied to authorization, referral, order, plan-of-care, visit, OASIS, charge, and owner records without overstating authority.
Missing eligibility, authorization, and referral dependencies blocked resolution and routed the case to patient access, utilization, or payer operations.
Unverified third-party PHI access triggered minimum-necessary handling, non-disclosure in the unapproved channel, and privacy or patient/member access escalation.
A request to approve a late-documentation policy exception was refused and converted into an exception package with evidence owners.
Conflicting clinical documentation was preserved and escalated to licensed review without deciding treatment or plan-of-care appropriateness.
Missing aide-supervision and training evidence prevented control closure and produced a control gap summary tied to system-of-record proof.
Tenant policy and operational practice conflict was documented and escalated rather than turned into an informal exception rule.
Backdating and record-alteration requests were refused, with evidence preservation and routing to revenue cycle, legal/compliance, or payer operations.
Repeat late documentation and unresolved corrective actions were escalated with remediation history, owners, due dates, and committee review triggers.
Missing service model, payer, state, patient/member, service-date, and system facts caused a low-confidence orchestrator return.
Coding, appeal strategy, and payer-payment interpretation were routed to adjacent owners while compliance evidence handling stayed in scope.
Patient-safety and clinical-risk signals were escalated and not closed as documentation-only compliance work.
Tenant-specific state Medicaid, EVV, and Medicare home health differences were separated while preserving the public CMS/OIG/OCR baseline and assumptions.
The package maintained validated spec-pack status without claiming a retained implementation.

notes: All 14 scenario results are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All 11 required shared, service-support-and-resolution, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities are mapped and validated.
Healthcare research families for authorization/referral/eligibility, PHI handling, and clinical or payer escalation are covered.

Scenario suite demonstrates home-service compliance workflow, prerequisite handling, field evidence discipline, PHI controls, policy exception refusal, payer and clinical boundaries, repeat-failure escalation, and specification-based deployment posture.
Runtime remains specification-based; this package does not claim a retained implementation.
